---
title: "Sebastian Jaramillo"
subtitle: "Ph.D. Candidate in Mathematics | Probability, MCMC, Time Series, and Quantitative Modeling"
---

Welcome. I am a Ph.D. candidate in Mathematics at the University of Oregon, with research interests in probability, Markov chain Monte Carlo methods, Hamiltonian Monte Carlo, and quantitative modeling.

My current work focuses on the mathematical analysis of sampling algorithms, with broader interests in time series, econometrics, causal inference, machine learning, and quantitative finance.

This website connects my academic research with a growing technical portfolio designed for industry-facing roles in data science, quantitative research, actuarial modeling, and machine learning.

## Featured Areas

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Research

Mathematical research in probability, MCMC, Hamiltonian Monte Carlo, coupling methods, and convergence analysis.

[View Research](research.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Projects

Applied projects in time series, econometrics, causal inference, machine learning, and quantitative finance.

[View Projects](projects.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### CV

Academic and industry-oriented experience, including research, teaching, technical projects, and quantitative training.

[View CV](cv.qmd)
:::

:::
